title: Quantum Optics Group Exploration
summary: Explore a UofT quantum optics lab to understand experimental photonics pathways.
detailed_description: This branch points students toward publicly listed UofT quantum and optics research groups where they can read publications, understand active projects, and prepare focused outreach.
eligibility: Open to students building research readiness; strongest fit for physics, EngSci, and ECE-adjacent interests.
quick_actions: Draft one question for a professor
Bookmark a seminar or reading list

title: Photonics and Optical Systems Course Track
source: University of Toronto Engineering Course Listings
summary: Build optics and hardware intuition through UofT course planning.
detailed_description: This path packages photonics-relevant UofT coursework into a concrete academic move that makes later lab or startup exploration more believable.
eligibility: Best fit for engineering and physics students planning deeper hardware work.
quick_actions: Add one course to your planning sheet

title: Temerty AI in Health Research Lead
source: University of Toronto Temerty Faculty of Medicine
summary: Explore publicly listed UofT health-AI labs and translational research opportunities.
detailed_description: A UofT student interested in healthcare AI can use this node to identify clinically grounded labs, understand their focus, and prepare stronger research-fit conversations.
eligibility: Good fit for CS, statistics, life sciences, biomedical engineering, and health studies students.
quick_actions: List one healthcare problem you care about

title: Data Sciences Institute Workshop Path
source: University of Toronto Data Sciences Institute
summary: Use UofT workshops and community events to deepen AI skills in a low-friction way.
detailed_description: This node highlights public UofT data science programming that helps students explore methods, meet peers, and find stronger next steps without overcommitting too early.
eligibility: Open to students across campuses and programs with data or AI curiosity.
quick_actions: Write down one topic to practice next week

title: Blue Sky Solar Racing Design Team
source: University of Toronto Student Design Teams
summary: Join a UofT design team to build visible technical experience and teamwork.
detailed_description: Design teams are a high-signal way to grow technical confidence, learn collaboration, and create portfolio proof inside the UofT ecosystem.
eligibility: Strong fit for engineering students and interdisciplinary builders who want applied work.
quick_actions: Prepare one short intro about your interests

title: Robotics Institute Research Cluster
summary: Explore UofT robotics labs, seminars, and faculty to build a research-facing robotics path.
detailed_description: This node centers robotics students on publicly visible UofT labs and faculty, making it easier to compare manipulation, autonomy, controls, and perception pathways.
eligibility: Best fit for engineering, CS, and math students interested in embodied systems.

title: Creative Destruction Lab Startup Path
source: University of Toronto Rotman / Creative Destruction Lab
summary: Connect technical interests to UofT's startup and venture ecosystem.
detailed_description: This node helps UofT students translate research or technical curiosity into entrepreneurship-adjacent experiences through founder events, venture programs, and startup literacy.
eligibility: Good fit for students interested in startups, commercialization, or venture-backed problem spaces.
quick_actions: Write one problem statement you care about

title: Rotman Fintech and Analytics Community
source: University of Toronto Rotman School of Management
summary: Use the UofT fintech community to test interest before committing to a career branch.
detailed_description: This node directs students toward student-led and official Rotman-facing fintech opportunities that help them build network density and industry vocabulary.
eligibility: Best fit for Rotman, economics, CS, and math students exploring finance + tech.
quick_actions: Map one fintech subfield you want to explore

title: Work-Study Research Support Role
source: University of Toronto Career Learning Network
summary: Turn exploration into a paid campus role through work-study and research support positions.
detailed_description: This path helps UofT students bridge curiosity and commitment by using campus work-study roles to gain professional proof, relationships, and domain familiarity.
eligibility: Depends on work-study eligibility and campus-specific posting windows.
quick_actions: Prepare a short tailored resume summary

title: UTM Innovation and Entrepreneurship Hub
summary: A UTM-focused branch for students exploring innovation, startup programs, and campus community building.
detailed_description: This node gives UTM students locally relevant entrepreneurial and experiential pathways rather than defaulting to St. George-centric options.
eligibility: Especially relevant for UTM students wanting campus-proximate opportunities.
quick_actions: Compare one campus program to a downtown alternative
Write down your preferred opportunity type

title: UTSC Co-op Technology Preparation
summary: Prepare for UTSC co-op and career pathways with stronger skill and opportunity targeting.
detailed_description: This node helps UTSC students translate broad interest into concrete co-op readiness through opportunity scanning, skill-gap awareness, and campus-specific supports.
eligibility: Best fit for UTSC students exploring industry-facing opportunities.
quick_actions: Book time to update your resume

title: Sustainability and Civic Innovation Community
summary: Build an impact-oriented path through UofT community, sustainability, and leadership opportunities.
detailed_description: This node offers a values-driven branch for students who want their academic path to connect to sustainability, social impact, and campus leadership.
eligibility: Open to students who want mission-driven experiences alongside academics.
quick_actions: Choose one impact theme to focus on
